name: Black Triangle
description: A flat equilateral plate, matte black or near-black, with three bright white or amber lights at the corners and a red pulsating light at the center. Reported sizes range from 30 meters to over 200 meters. Always silent or near-silent. Moves at low altitude, sometimes hovering, sometimes drifting at low speed. The three corner lights are diagnostic — the central red pulse is the secondary signature. The hull is essentially invisible in darkness; the craft reads as three lights in triangular formation.

ir_signature.ir_notes: Night-sky star-occlusion is diagnostic — the hull blocks stars behind it, confirming solid body. IR imagery rare; Belgian F-16 radar confirmed solid return.

cultural_context: The black triangle is the second most culturally prominent UAP type after the saucer. The 1989–90 Belgian Wave — 2,000+ witnesses, multiple police officers, two F-16 intercept attempts, the iconic (later confessed-fake) Petit-Rechain photograph — locked the equilateral plate + 3-amber-lights grammar into public consciousness. Phoenix Lights 1997 reinforced it at mass scale. Frequently associated with TR-3B reverse-engineering theories.
